title: lamprococcus brevicollis
intro: Lamprococcus brevicollis, also known as the "shrubby bushwillow", is a type of shrub native to southern Africa. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Lamprococcus brevicollis include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
